name: Salisbury steak
author: Chef John
description: One of the most popular TV dinner entrees of that era was the Salisbury steak, and this is my not-too-modern take on that classic dish.

step_sections.name: Steak patties
steps: Mix the ground beef, freshly ground black pepper, dry mustard, 2 tablespoons tomato ketchup, 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, egg, cayenne, and plain breadcrumbs with a fork until thoroughly combined.
Pat the mixture down, wrap it up, and place in the fridge for at least a few hours or overnight.
Moisten hands with water and form the mixture into four patties about 1/2 inch thick.

step_sections.name: Gravy
steps: Melt 2 tablespoons of butter over high heat in a large non-stick pan.
Add sliced mushrooms, diced onion, and a pinch of salt. Saute until nicely browned.
Stir in flour and cook for about 3 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon ketchup, a little Worcestershire sauce, and 3 cups beef broth.
Bring to a simmer over high heat, then reduce to medium and simmer for about 10 minutes until thickened.
Transfer gravy to a bowl and reserve.

step_sections.name: Cooking
steps: Heat a couple tablespoons of butter in the pan used for the gravy over medium-high heat.
Brown the patties for 2 to 3 minutes per side.
Transfer the gravy back into the pan with the patties.
Once simmering, back heat down to medium and cook for about 5 more minutes, basting with a spoon, until cooked through.
